tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Brent surged 13% to peak; now at $83.50/bbl (Day 5) with sustained geopolitical risk premium hardening. Goldman Sachs models $18/bbl 'Iran shock' add-on; BloombergNEF targets $91/bbl under prolonged disruption. ASEAN energy producers (PTTEP, Medco Energi, Sapura Energy) benefit directly from price spike. 23% of global LNG trade transits Hormuz — Qatar's 77 mtpa effectively offline. Long near-dated Brent vs short deferred as backwardation steepens. Entered at $79; now $83.50; base target $106, stress target $125. OPEC+ March 4 response (+206k bpd) confirmed strategic restraint — no meaningful supply offset while Hormuz blocked. Trump naval escort program announced but operational scope too limited to meaningfully restore transit volumes.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: ASEAN Banks index at 213.5 (-1.3% on Mar 4) — continuing selloff on credit widening concerns; net-oil-exporting names (Malaysia, Singapore banks) relatively insulated. ASEAN Energy Equities at 137.2 (+1.0%) bid on oil spike continuing. iTraxx Asia IG at 138bp (+6bp on Mar 4) signals credit still widening; ASEAN HY at 444bp (+12bp). MUFG flags MYR and SGD bank balance sheets as relatively insulated vs PHP/IDR. Recommend maintaining bank long for rate-cut delay beneficiaries. Wilmar and IJM plantation equity longs complement as oil-correlated ASEAN plays.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: PHP most vulnerable ASEAN currency per MUFG (Michael Wan, Mar 3). USD/PHP at 58.62 (Mar 4, +0.5% on session) with 1,400+ OFWs requesting repatriation adding remittance income uncertainty. >50% of Philippines power generation relies on imported fossil fuels; no strategic LNG reserves. Philippines current account deficit widens to -4.5% of GDP at $90/bbl. BSP rate cut cycle now on hold indefinitely — but IDR and THB also pressured, narrowing yield support. MUFG stress target: 54.50 on sustained oil spike implies near-term risk to 59–60 range. JKM LNG at $16.80 and rising compounds LNG import cost exposure. Stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 16,910 (Day 5 of Iran shock, -0.5% on Mar 4 — modest IDR recovery on safe-haven dollar rotation easing). Net oil importer exposed to Hormuz-driven price surge; Bank Indonesia forced into defensive rate hold at 4.75% despite growth pressure. Indonesia energy minister announced March 4 shift of crude import orders from Middle East to US amid Hormuz closure — confirming medium-term supply cost inflation. Indonesian-branded crude surging into China (~291,000 bpd) covering Iranian origin — OFAC enforcement tightening under MAS February 2026 FATF guidance. Target 17,500–18,000 in stress. Brent at $83.50 baseline; stress target $100 accelerates IDR trajectory.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 138bp (+6bp on Mar 4, continuing to grind wider). Iran shock + simultaneous SCS Heightened risk creates dual-front credit deterioration. CDS entered at 102bp on pre-shock level — now deep in-the-money. Day 5 widening continues: all 12 P&I clubs exiting tonight (effective March 5) confirms de facto blockade permanence. Hold existing 5Y position; 180–220bp is credible stress target in 1–3 month Hormuz disruption scenario. Sapura Energy and Hyflux bonds are the most vulnerable ASEAN HY names. ASEAN HY Composite at 444bp (+12bp) continuing to widen.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $16.80/MMBtu (+7.7% on Mar 4) as Asian buyers scramble with Qatar Hormuz supply offline and QatarEnergy force majeure at Ras Laffan confirmed. TTF at €52.80/MWh (+54.4% cumulative from pre-QatarEnergy-halt levels) — Atlantic Basin LNG re-routing has limits. 84% of Hormuz LNG goes to Asia; JKM/TTF spread continuing to widen on Day 5, confirming Asian supply squeeze is structural under Hormuz closure. Wilmar (palm oil / biodiesel competitor to LNG) and PTTEP (LNG producer beneficiary) are key client-linked expressions of this theme. In 1-month Hormuz closure, JKM could approach $25/MMBtu. Long JKM outright or long JKM vs short TTF captures asymmetric Asian supply squeeze.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 represent an acute refinancing risk under current market conditions. iTraxx Asia IG at 138bp and ASEAN HY at 444bp signal the window is still open for secured bank refinancing but narrowing rapidly day by day. E&P capex clients recovering above $83.50/bbl Brent threshold, but capex lag means Sapura revenue uplift is 2–3 quarters behind oil price. High leverage (net debt ~MYR 10bn) and MAS February 2026 FATF guidance on Iran sanctions create compounding risk — bilateral bank discussions must commence immediately before P&I exit (effective midnight March 5) further tightens credit appetite.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — BOT rate hold (inflation pressure from Brent at $83.50) means pre-financing at current 3.18% TH 10Y is the optimal window. Iran shock creates oil revenue timing mismatch: PTTEP's upstream EBITDA improves with oil price, but THB bond repayment is fixed. Recommend pre-financing THB bond now while rates remain below 3.25% and before any BOT inflation repricing or THB depreciation (now at 31.70) adds to cost. MAS February 2026 FATF guidance on Iran sanctions may affect Gulf of Oman JV compliance — complete jurisdictional review in parallel.

Wilmar's USD revolving credit facility should be expanded to accommodate oil-driven input cost inflation. Bunker fuel represents ~12% of cost base — at $83.50 Brent, the annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 is est. USD 340–400M. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) improves EBITDA but cash conversion lag creates working capital pressure. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 500–750M recommended before commodity trade finance bank appetite tightens further under Iran secondary sanctions review. iTraxx Asia IG at 138bp is still an executable pricing level but window narrowing as P&I exit tonight drives risk repricing.

Energy cost relief window: if Hormuz de-escalates within 4–6 weeks (Base scenario, 38% probability and declining), Brent could retrace toward $68–72 — creating a brief refinancing window for Hyflux's restructuring creditors. Restructuring plan repayment milestones fall due H2 2026; ASEAN HY at 444bp effectively closes the capital markets window for unsecured debt. Explore alternative private credit, DFI, or multilateral bridge financing. Engage restructuring advisor immediately to model refinancing window scenarios contingent on oil trajectory. Energy cost escalation covenant must be reviewed vs restructuring plan assumptions made at $55–60/bbl.
